headline: A reported US government shutdown of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 drives model regulation to the top of the window.
body: Multiple items describe Fable 5 and Mythos 5 pulled from availability by US government order after roughly three days, with commentary citing export-control directives, jailbreak concerns, and a claimed extension of the ban into Europe. Model regulation, frontier models, and model capability all broke out alongside the story, framing questions about whether top models stay public or move to restricted access. In parallel, an inference-speed race surfaced: Xiaomi's MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed pairs FP4 quantization, speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to clear 1,000 tokens per second, and Google's DiffusionGemma hits similar throughput by shifting text generation from memory-bound to compute-bound through diffusion. Speculative decoding, path kernel, and memory-bound vs compute-bound entered as new tooling concepts.

cited_items.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
